headline: Survey at 0.6% completion; urgent acceleration needed across 8 regions to meet 1,350 target.
collection_status: Only 8 responses collected against 1,350 target represents 0.6% progress. Data concentrated in 2 of 10 regions (MARA, SONGWE) with heavy volunteer skew (75%). Current pace will not meet objectives without immediate intervention.
key_findings: Volunteer-dominant sample (6/8) risks bias; only 2 RC responses suggests partner engagement is critically low across programme structure
Geographic coverage severely limited: MARA (5) and SONGWE (3) account for 100% of responses; 8 regions completely unrepresented
Priority consensus exists on programme expansion: new_programs (5/8) and expand_kf (3/8) indicate stakeholder appetite for scaling despite low satisfaction baseline (scale_readiness: 3.0/10)
Qualitative feedback emphasizes system sustainability: teacher incentives, government policy integration, digital monitoring, and multi-level coordination are recurrent themes across all 8 responses
flags: CRITICAL: 99.4% of target responses outstanding; current collection rate (8 responses) unsustainable for deadline—requires 3–4x acceleration immediately
COVERAGE GAP: 8 regions dormant; MARA and SONGWE monopolizing data suggests regional coordinators in other zones are non-functional or disengaged
SAMPLING RISK: 75% volunteer composition distorts findings; RC perspective under-represented, risking skewed programme recommendations
recommendation: Immediately audit and activate regional coordinator networks in all 10 regions with targeted outreach targets and weekly response tracking. Implement stratified quota system (min. 40% RC respondents) to rebalance sample and ensure representative feedback on partnership readiness before proceeding with expansion strategy.
